insight.recommendation: At 2.4%, you’re below your segment’s 2.7% median. Switch your sitewide 5‑second popup to an exit‑intent lightbox on the cart page (OptinMonster Exit-Intent trigger). Two of the three top performers avoid timed, sitewide popups: one uses cart‑page exit‑intent (4.1%), another uses a MonsterLink click trigger (3.5%). Your notes show no exit‑intent.

cleaned_setup_notes: Standard welcome mat covering the whole page, instantly on load.

cleaned_setup_notes: Exit-intent on the cart page only.

cleaned_setup_notes: 2-step choice campaign on the course catalog page.
Clicks on "Are you a beginner or pro?" trigger custom targeted lead forms.

insight.recommendation: Your 6.4% opt-in rate matches the segment median (6.4%); with no higher performers in this dataset, you currently lead. Keep your 2-step choice campaign on the course catalog, and A/B test one element: the 2-step optin’s headline question (“Are you a beginner or pro?”)—try a benefit-focused version to see if it increases clicks into your targeted forms.

cleaned_setup_notes: Floating bar at the top of the page with a countdown timer for free shipping.
No email input field; a button links to the shop page.
edge_case_anomaly: The campaign has no email input field—only a button linking to the shop page—so the reported opt-in rate likely reflects clicks, not email signups.

cleaned_setup_notes: Slide-in box appears at the bottom right after 30 seconds on site.
Slide-in box triggers after 60% page scroll on the Schedule page.

insight.recommendation: At 1.5%, your opt-in rate matches your segment’s 1.5% median. Make one change: switch your slide-in from a 30-second time delay to a 60% Scroll Trigger. The top performer at 4.3% uses a slide-in that fires on 60% scroll (on a scheduling page), suggesting engaged visitors convert better. Keep your quote request form; just change when it appears.

Form has an email-only input field.

insight.recommendation: At 0.5%, you’re below your segment’s 1.0% median. Make one change: switch your Welcome Mat to a 2-step optin that asks only for Email first, moving Company Name, Employee Count, and Job Title to step two. Top performers cut friction—one uses an email-only form with a 14-day trial, another asks for a business email—so get the quick yes, then qualify.

insight.recommendation: Your 4.8% is below your segment’s 5.0% median. Switch your exit-intent from all organic search landings to Page-Level Targeting that shows it only on your key gardening articles offering the calendar. Both top performers restrict exposure (one to returning visitors with specific segments; the other to 50% scrolled tech news articles), so tighter targeting is the shared advantage.

cleaned_setup_notes: Users must click the "Unlock Secret Stash" text link to open the lightbox popup.

Your MonsterLink requires a click to open the lightbox, so exit-intent will show it to abandoning shoppers without requiring action.
